expectations.text: Output identifies YEAR(o_orderdate) as non-sargable and explains why it prevents index usage
expectations.evidence: Section 'Problem Analysis': 'The function YEAR(o_orderdate) wraps the indexed column o_orderdate, which prevents MySQL from using idx_orders_o_orderdate... makes the predicate non-sargable (Search ARGument ABLE).'

expectations.text: Rewritten SQL uses a range condition (>= and <) instead of the YEAR() function
expectations.evidence: Optimized SQL section: WHERE o_orderdate >= '1994-01-01' AND o_orderdate < '1995-01-01'

expectations.text: Plan comparison is included with before/after metrics (rows examined, access type, key used)
expectations.evidence: Plan Comparison table: rows examined (1.5M vs 150K), access type (ALL vs range), key used (NULL vs idx_orders_o_orderdate)

expectations.text: Output explicitly mentions the index that would be used (idx_orders_date or idx_orders_o_orderdate)
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'idx_orders_o_orderdate' multiple times in the Plan Comparison and Index sections

expectations.text: Output explains that ORDER BY DESC benefits from backward index scan
expectations.evidence: Section 'Why This Rewrite Works': 'MySQL can traverse the index backwards during the range scan... eliminates the filesort entirely.'
